नई दिल्ली
NEW DELHI
याचिका संख्या./ Petition No. 78/MP/2023
कोरम/ Coram:
श्री जिश्न ु बरुआ, अध्यक्ष/Shri Jishnu Barua, Chairperson
श्री रमेश बाब ू वी., सिस्य/Shri Ramesh Babu V., Member
श्री हरीश ििु ानी, सिस्य/Shri Harish Dudani, Member
श्री रवविं र ससहं दिल्लों, सिस्य/Shri Ravinder Singh Dhillon, ...

c) Since the notifications dated 30.09.2021 in regard to GST Laws were notified prior to
Change in Law Rules (notified on 22.10.2021), these events shall be ‘dealt in accordance
with the prevalent dispensation/ rule position at the time of occurrence of the event’ and
not as per the Change in Law Ru...

Re. Requirement to furnish relevant documents and thereby establish one to one
correlation
i) The Petitioner is required to establish a one to one correlation between the project, the
supply of goods against which change in law is claimed, the invoices and other relevant
documents for proof of the p...

Re. Cut-off date for payment of claim amount on account of GST
k) In the event of occurrence of any of events as provided under Article 12.1.1 or Article
12.1.3 which results in any increase/ decrease in the Project Cost, subject to the condition
that such Change in Law is recognised by the Appropri...

o) As per Article 12.1.3 of the PPA, the quantum of payment in respect of the evaluated
change in law claims of the Petitioner, inter-alia, on account of change in rates of GST
after 22.06.2020 shall be provided as per Article 12.2.3 of the PPA subject to fulfilment
of conditions prescribed in Artic...

u) It is settled principle that in the matters of restitution, the courts should adopt pragmatic
view and grant relief in a manner as may be reasonable, fair and practicable.

It has been
Order in Petition No. 78/MP/2023 Page 10 of 31held that the Court should not be oblivious of any unmerited hardship to be suffered by
the party against whom action by way of restitution is taken.

v) The Petitioner should be required to establish to the satisfaction of the Commission that
it has made prudent and bona-fide effort to minimise the interest cost.

b) Article 12.1.3 of the PPA dated 20.08.2021 signed between the Petitioner and SECI
squarely provides that change in rates of safeguard duty, GST and basic customs duty
after 22.06.2020 shall be treated as change in law “subject to the provision that
Appropriate Commission recognizes such provision...

c) However, the Commission vide order dated 08.03.2022 had not made such a recognition,
relying upon the Electricity (Timely Recovery of Costs due to Change in Law) Rules 2021
(CIL Rules, 2021), notified by the Ministry of Power (MoP) on 22.10.2021, wherein on
occurrence of a Change in Law, the affe...

m) As regards the rate of interest, SECI has contended that Carrying Cost is to be restricted
to the cost of financing of a prudent utility and that the Commission should adopt a
pragmatic view.

9 and 10;
(b) Approve Trading Margin of Rs.0.07/kWh as agreed to by the Distribution
Companies in the signed PSAs in terms of Regulation 8 (1) (d) of the Trading
License Regulations, 2020; and
(c) Recognize, in terms of Article 12.2 of the PPAs and Article 8.2 of the PSAs,
that the change in rates o...

However, the recognition of Basic Custom Duty on Solar
Modules and Cells as a Change in Law event shall be subject to issuance of
appropriate notification by Ministry of Finance, Government of India.

(h) During the pendency of the above Petition, the Central Government, in
exercise of powers under Section 176 of the Act, has on 22.10.2021 notified
the Electricity (Timely Recovery of Costs due to Change in Law) Rules, 2021
(hereinafter referred to as “the Change in Law Rules”).

The Respondent No. 4, ERBPL vide its written submissions dated 4.2.2022 has
additionally submitted as under:
…
(f) While it has been admitted by the Petitioner that the Change in Law Rules
provide for adjustment in tariff on occurrence of Change in Law and specifically
entitle the affected party to ...

As per sub-rule (2) of Rule 1, these Rules shall come into
force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.

The change in law events occurred prior to the notification of
these Rules shall be dealt in accordance with the prevalent dispensation /rule
position at the time of occurrence of the event.

The proceedings in the petitions, related to change in law matters, pending
before the Appropriate Commissions, shall be dealt in accordance with
stipulations made in para (ii) above.

For the foregoing reasons, we find the impugned orders of the Central
Commission applying the CIL Rules to matters pending before it for adjudication
under Section 79(1)(f) of Electricity Act on the date of coming into force of said rules
wholly erroneous, improper and bad in law.

The
Central Commission is duty-bound to consider each of them on the merits of the
claims and adjudicate in accordance with law on the dispute(s) in proper exercise of
its jurisdiction under Section 79 of the Electricity Act.
